---
title: ""
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="32%"}
![](<pedro_portrait/side_caffee_pm.jpg>){.headshot width="210px"}

<p style="margin-top:0.6em; margin-bottom:0;">
<strong style="font-size:1.05em;">Pedro Masi</strong><br>
<span style="font-size:0.88em;">Information Systems PhD Student</span><br>
<span style="font-size:0.88em;">📍 Knoxville, TN</span><br>
<span style="font-size:0.88em;">University of Tennessee</span>
</p>

<p style="font-size:0.85em; line-height:2.0; margin-top:0.6em;">
[CV (PDF)](cv.qmd)<br>
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pedro-masi/)<br>
[UTK Profile](https://haslam.utk.edu/people/profile/pedro-masi/)<br>
📧 [pmmasig@gmail.com](mailto:pmmasig@gmail.com)
</p>
:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

::: {.column width="64%"}

## Biography

I am a PhD Student in Business Analytics and Statistics at the Haslam College of Business, University of Tennessee, Knoxville. My research lies at the intersection of AI and causal inference. I apply text mining and embedding techniques to measure technology adoption and its impact on labor and financial outcomes, converting unstructured patent and job-posting text into structured representations that feed econometric models estimating the effects of AI exposure on skill value and firm capital investments.

**Research Fields**

- Causal Inference 
- Machine Learning & Representation Learning
- Natural Language Processing (NLP)

**Education**

- PhD, Business Analytics and Statistics — University of Tennessee, Knoxville (2023–Present)
- M.S., Finance and Technology — Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (2020–2021)
- M.S., Agricultural Economics — Kansas State University (2017–2019)
- B.A., Agricultural Economics — Kansas State University (2011–2015)

**Advisors:** Chuanren Liu, Yuanyang Liu

**Job Market:** I am on the 2026–2027 academic job market.

:::

::::
